meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Azure MLOps Engineer Associate (AI-300)
meta.title: MS-AI300-5: Pipelines, Registries, and Production Monitoring

questions.stem: Which Azure Machine Learning compute target is primarily intended for individual development and notebook work?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a compute instance is the managed single-node environment for development and notebooks. A compute cluster is for scalable job execution, while endpoints and registries serve different purposes.

questions.stem: A training job must scale out when demand increases and scale back to zero when idle. Which compute choice is best?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because compute clusters are designed for autoscaling job workloads. Compute instances are personal development machines, and the other services are not training compute.

questions.stem: Which asset type is best when a pipeline step needs tabular data plus schema and transformation logic?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because MLTable is built for structured data definitions and access patterns in Azure ML. The other options are not data asset formats.

questions.stem: Which endpoint type is designed for low-latency request/response scoring?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because managed online endpoints are optimized for synchronous low-latency inference. Batch endpoints are for asynchronous large-scale scoring.

questions.stem: A team must score millions of records overnight and write results in bulk. Which deployment pattern fits best?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because batch endpoints are designed for large asynchronous scoring jobs. Online endpoints are optimized for real-time calls.

questions.stem: What is the main reason to use an Azure ML registry?

options.text: To share and version models, components, and environments across workspaces

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because registries centralize reusable ML assets across workspaces. They do not replace compute, serve only logs, or remove governance.

questions.stem: What is a pipeline component in Azure ML?

options.text: A reusable, versioned step definition with declared inputs and outputs
A dedicated GPU attached to a notebook

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because components package a reusable job step with interface and runtime definitions. The other choices are unrelated.

questions.stem: Which MLflow call logs a numeric evaluation value such as accuracy?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because `log_metric` records numeric metrics. `log_param` records configuration values, and the others perform different MLflow tasks.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: A team wants to shift 10% of real-time traffic to a new model before full cutover. Which Azure ML capability supports this best?

options.text: Multiple deployments behind one online endpoint with traffic splitting

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because managed online endpoints support multiple deployments and controlled traffic allocation. The other options do not provide live traffic splitting.

questions.stem: Which tool in Azure ML helps investigate fairness, error patterns, and model behavior before release?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the Responsible AI dashboard is designed for interpretability, fairness, and error analysis workflows. The other options do not provide model behavior assessment.

questions.stem: Which TWO items are commonly declared in a command component YAML definition? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because components declare inputs, outputs, command, environment, and related runtime settings. The other choices are not component-interface fields.

questions.stem: Why should a team log hyperparameters during training runs?

options.text: To compare runs and reproduce training behavior later

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because logged hyperparameters support comparison and reproducibility across runs. They do not improve endpoint throughput or replace registry and data management.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: What is the benefit of versioning environments in Azure ML?

options.text: It makes training and deployment dependencies reproducible
It removes the need for inference code

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because environment versioning preserves the exact dependency set used for jobs and deployments. The other claims are incorrect.

questions.stem: Which file pair is commonly used for custom online deployments in Azure ML to define inference behavior?

options.text: A scoring script and an environment definition
A failover group and a masking policy
A vector index and an embedding model

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because online deployments typically rely on scoring logic plus an environment/image definition. The other pairs belong to other platforms or purposes.

questions.stem: Why is monitoring production predictions against later ground truth valuable?

options.text: It helps detect performance drift over time
It removes the need for deployment testing

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because comparing predictions with realized outcomes helps detect degradation and drift. The other claims overstate what monitoring can do.

questions.stem: A team wants a training workflow that can be rerun with different parameters but the same step structure. What should they build?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because parameterized pipelines support repeatable execution with different runtime values. The other choices do not orchestrate reusable training workflows.

questions.stem: Which artifact is most useful for reproducing the exact model produced by a historical run?

options.text: The run's code, parameters, environment, and logged outputs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because reproducibility depends on the full run context: code, inputs, parameters, environment, and outputs. The other items alone are insufficient.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: What does model registration primarily provide?

options.text: A named, versioned record of a model for promotion and deployment
A way to encrypt every dataset

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because registration creates a versioned model asset used in lifecycle management. The other features serve different purposes.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about batch endpoints are true? (Select TWO)

options.text: They are intended for asynchronous large-scale scoring
They can read input data and write output in bulk
They are the main choice for interactive low-latency chat
They require every request to return in milliseconds

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because batch endpoints are built for large asynchronous inference workflows. **C** and **D** describe online-serving expectations instead.

questions.stem: A team can explain predictions, but it still cannot tell whether one subgroup has much higher error than another. Which Responsible AI capability is most relevant?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because subgroup-oriented error analysis helps reveal uneven model performance. The other capabilities do not analyze fairness or cohort errors.

questions.stem: Which Azure ML feature helps package dependencies once and reuse them across jobs?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because environment assets package dependencies for repeatable job execution. The other items are unrelated.

questions.stem: When would `mlflow.log_param()` be more appropriate than `mlflow.log_metric()`?

options.text: When recording a training setting such as `max_depth=8`
When recording validation AUC after each epoch
When exporting a model to an endpoint

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because parameters describe run configuration, while metrics capture numeric performance results. The other actions are not MLflow parameter logging.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: Why might an organization store approved components and environments in a registry instead of only in one workspace?

options.text: To promote standardized, governed reuse across multiple workspaces
To eliminate the need for source control
To turn online endpoints into batch jobs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because registries enable centralized governed reuse. The other answers are incorrect.

questions.stem: What is the best reason to include input and output schema expectations with a model deployment?

options.text: They make serving behavior clearer and reduce integration errors
They remove the need for authentication

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because clear interface expectations reduce downstream integration mistakes. They do not replace monitoring, retraining, or authentication.

questions.stem: Which TWO signals suggest a model should be retrained or investigated? (Select TWO)

options.text: Accuracy or business KPI drops after deployment
Input data distribution shifts materially from training
A new notebook kernel is available

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because performance decline and input drift are standard retraining triggers. The other options are irrelevant.

questions.stem: A pipeline step should run only after an upstream component produces a model output. What mechanism provides that dependency?

options.text: Wiring the downstream input to the upstream output in the pipeline graph
Increasing endpoint traffic to the step
Assigning both steps to the same studio user

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because pipeline dataflow dependencies are expressed by connecting outputs to inputs. The other choices do not enforce execution order.

questions.stem: What is a key reason to keep data assets versioned?

options.text: So experiments can be traced back to the exact data used
So endpoint traffic can be split

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because versioned data assets support lineage and reproducibility. The other outcomes are unrelated.

questions.stem: A team promotes a model from development to production. What should remain consistent to minimize surprises?

options.text: The environment and serving contract used to validate the model

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because consistent dependencies and interface assumptions reduce promotion risk. The other items do not affect model correctness.

questions.stem: What is the strongest reason to use a blue/green or canary-style rollout for online inference?

options.text: It reduces deployment risk by exposing the new model to limited traffic first
It guarantees the new model is more accurate
It eliminates the need for monitoring

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because incremental rollout limits blast radius while the team observes real behavior. The other statements are false.

questions.stem: Which practice best supports auditability in MLOps?

options.text: Keeping versioned assets, run history, and deployment records
Deleting old runs after each release

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because versioned assets and historical records support traceability and auditability. The other choices weaken governance.

questions.stem: Which TWO compute-related statements are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Compute instances are for interactive development
Compute clusters are for scalable job execution
Batch endpoints are notebook authoring environments

questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because they match Azure ML compute roles. **C** and **D** do not.

questions.stem: Why is a consistent experiment naming and tagging strategy useful?

options.text: It makes run comparison, search, and lineage easier
It removes the need for datasets
It is required to create any endpoint

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because naming and tags help organize and query run history. The other claims are false.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: An organization wants one central source for approved feature engineering components reused by many teams. What is the best fit?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because registries are intended for sharing governed ML assets across workspaces and teams. The other options are not central governed asset stores.

questions.stem: Which deployment pattern is the best default for real-time REST scoring in Azure ML?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because managed online endpoints provide the synchronous serving pattern for real-time REST calls. The others do not.

questions.stem: What is the primary value of a baseline captured before deploying a new model?

options.text: It gives a reference point for detecting future degradation
It removes the need for logs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a baseline allows later monitoring comparisons. The other statements are incorrect.

questions.stem: Which source-control automation path is officially supported for Azure ML workflows?

options.text: GitHub Actions orchestrating Azure ML jobs and deployments

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Microsoft documents GitHub Actions integration for Azure ML automation. The other options are not the official Azure ML CI/CD path here.

questions.stem: A team changes code, data version, and environment between runs. Which claim is safest?

options.text: Run-to-run comparisons are less reliable unless the changes are logged and understood
Comparisons stay fully valid without any metadata
Metrics no longer need to be stored

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because comparisons require tracked context for the changed code, data, and environment. The other answers ignore reproducibility basics.
questions.reference: [Log metrics, parameters, and files with MLflow - Azure Machine Learning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-log-view-metrics)

questions.stem: Which TWO items are reusable asset types often shared through Azure ML registries? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A** and **B** are correct because registries commonly store models, components, and environments for reuse. The other items are not registry asset types.

questions.stem: Why would a team prefer a batch endpoint over retraining inside a notebook loop?

options.text: Because the scoring workload is operationalized, scalable, and asynchronous
Because batch endpoints are interactive IDEs
Because notebooks cannot access data assets
Because batch endpoints automatically improve model quality

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because batch endpoints operationalize large scoring jobs in a scalable asynchronous manner. The other statements are false.

questions.stem: After rollout, who should have access to deployment and lineage records?

options.text: Appropriate operators and reviewers under least-privilege controls
No one once the model is live
Only people who authored the notebook

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because least-privilege access to deployment records balances oversight with security. The other choices are unsafe or impractical.
